title: Rc Shared Ownership
description: Rc<T> (Reference Counted) allows multiple owners of the same data. The data is freed when the last Rc is dropped.

steps.label: Create Rc
steps.annotation: a is an Rc pointing to heap data with reference count 1.

steps.label: Clone Rc (cheap!)
steps.code:
let b = Rc::clone(&a);
let c = Rc::clone(&a);
steps.annotation: Rc::clone doesn't copy the data — it only increments the reference count. All three point to the same heap data.

steps.label: Drop b and c
steps.code: // b, c go out of scope
steps.annotation: Each drop decrements the refcount. Data stays alive because a still holds a reference (count = 1).

steps.label: Drop a (last owner)
steps.annotation: The last Rc drops, refcount hits 0, and the heap data is finally freed. No garbage collector needed!
